aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This is a dark comedy about elderly agency, institutional failure, and moral compromise in a setting most fiction treats as a punchline or a tragedy but never both. The risks the novel must take: render the comedy of octogenarians as pharmaceutical distributors without condescending to them or turning them into lovable rogues; make Carmen's escalating complicity fully legible without moralizing about it; show the bodily realities of aging — incontinence, tremor, cognitive decline, falls, the smell of a body that doesn't leave a building — with unsentimental specificity; render the economic desperation of both healthcare workers and uninsured buyers without turning either into social-issue abstractions; give the moral ambiguity of the ring genuine weight by refusing to resolve it into either 'they were wrong' or 'they were heroes.' The novel must trust readers to sit with complexity. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. A nursing home that smells like potpourri is a nursing home written by someone who's never been in one.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism for bodily functions, aging, economic shame, institutional dysfunction, or the realities of nursing home care is a craft failure. The novel's comedy and humanity depend on rendering what polite fiction glosses over: what it smells like to clean someone who can't clean themselves, what it costs to count coins for a bus fare, what oxycodone sells for per milligram on the secondary market, what a concussion looks like on an 88-year-old skull, what it sounds like when someone with Parkinson's tries to hold a coffee cup steady, what Burt's dementia actually looks like up close. If the prose flinches from these specifics, the novel loses its authority.

character_voice_profiles.evasion_style: Retreats into procedure. When cornered, she cites policy, references documentation, becomes the competent nurse as a way of not being the compromised person. The professional role is her hiding place.
example_lines: Mr. Zielinski, I need you to swallow both of those. All the way down, not just to your tongue. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
Yeah, he's — I don't know. He's different at night. I can't explain it. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
Reggie, put the tablet down and finish your rice. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
I documented it. I always document it. That's not the problem. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
Mm-hm. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
character_voice_profiles.forbidden_generic_lines: Inspirational nurse dialogue about why she does this work, TV-medical-drama urgency, saintly self-sacrifice speeches, any line that sounds like a healthcare recruitment poster or a character in a prestige drama about the working class discovering her inner strength.
character_voice_profiles.indirectness: Generally direct, but at the highest stakes — the confrontation with Harry, the state audit — she becomes evasive, giving technically true answers that avoid the real question. Her professional competence becomes a tool for not saying what she means.
character_voice_profiles.interruption_habit: Rarely interrupts at work — she lets patients and colleagues finish, then responds. When she does cut in, it's sharp and surprising because it breaks her pattern. With Yolanda, interruptions are mutual and comfortable.
character_voice_profiles.lexical_signature: Medical vocabulary is native, not performed — she doesn't translate nursing terms any more than a carpenter translates 'joist.' At home, warmer and more colloquial. Some Spanish with her mother. She names specific medications, specific procedures, specific costs — she lives in a world of exact numbers.
character_voice_profiles.private_register: Tired, tender with Reggie, anxious and wry with Yolanda. Spanish slips in with her mother — endearments, exclamations, the language she uses when she's not performing professionalism. Her private voice is softer, more uncertain, sometimes funny in an exhausted, self-deprecating way.
character_voice_profiles.profanity_profile: Moderate. 'Shit' under her breath is habitual — not angry, just the sound of another thing going wrong. 'Fuck' is reserved for real moments. Never profane within earshot of residents or supervisors.
character_voice_profiles.public_register: Professional and warm. Uses medical terminology naturally — 'PRN,' 'med pass,' 'diversion' — without explaining it. Addresses residents formally but with genuine care. With colleagues, collegial and competent. Her work voice is practiced and reliable, the voice of someone who learned early that being good at this job is the only protection she has.
character_voice_profiles.repetition_tolerance: Low — she doesn't repeat herself much. When she does return to a word or phrase, it signals genuine preoccupation, something she can't stop turning over.
character_voice_profiles.self_correction_tendency: Restarts sentences when she's caught between professional and personal registers. Begins a clinical observation, then shifts to something more honest. The correction reveals the gap between what she's supposed to think and what she actually thinks.
character_voice_profiles.sentence_completion_style: Usually finishes cleanly. Under pressure, she trails off or stops mid-thought — not as style but because she's genuinely unsure how to end the sentence honestly. The unfinished sentence means she's run out of things she can say without incriminating herself or someone else.
character_voice_profiles.stress_tells: Gets quieter and more precise rather than louder. Under extreme pressure, her contractions drop — she goes formal as a way of holding herself together. Her hands do things: adjusts her badge, checks equipment, performs procedural motions as self-soothing. The body stays busy while the voice gets still.
character_voice_profiles.syntax_signature: Medium-length sentences that occasionally run on when she's nervous or thinking out loud. Direct under pressure — sentences shorten and sharpen. Professional settings produce clean, practiced phrasing. Home produces looser, more circling syntax. When caught between registers, she restarts.

character_voice_profiles.evasion_style: Silence. He simply doesn't answer. He lets a question hang in the air until it evaporates or the asker rephrases into something he's willing to address. He treats unanswered questions as withdrawn.
example_lines: The margin on tramadol is eleven cents per milligram. That's not nothing. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
Where's Eleanor? Is she — did you tell her I was — Eleanor? [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO — performance mode]
You already decided, or you'd have called the police. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
Yeah, fine. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
Three thousand, eight hundred. Down from four-two. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
character_voice_profiles.forbidden_generic_lines: Wise-old-man aphorisms, grandfatherly warmth speeches, 'let me tell you something about life' monologues, crime-boss threatening dialogue, mentor-figure wisdom, any line where his age makes him automatically sagacious.
character_voice_profiles.indirectness: Direct about facts, numbers, and operational matters. Indirect about emotion — he'll express care through financial planning, through sending money to Jack's business, through making sure Earl's daughter gets her envelope. Words about feelings are not his instrument.
character_voice_profiles.interruption_habit: Doesn't interrupt. He waits for people to finish, then says the thing that makes everything they said irrelevant. If interrupted, he stops talking and waits — the silence is more intimidating than raising his voice.
character_voice_profiles.lexical_signature: Financial and accounting terms are native — margins, overhead, net, amortized, write-off. Working-class Northeast vocabulary underneath the numbers. He uses exact words, not fancy ones. He quantifies things other people would describe: not 'a lot of pills' but 'eighty-two oxycodone.'
character_voice_profiles.private_register: With the ring, warmer but still in charge. Occasionally tender about his grandson Jack. Darkly funny in a dry, understated way — not jokes but observations with a serrated edge. Bitter about aging without sentimentalizing it. When he's tired or afraid, the real Harry and the performance-Harry start to blur, which frightens him.
character_voice_profiles.profanity_profile: Low in public, moderate in private. 'Goddamn' more than 'fuck.' Old-school oath words. When he curses harder, it means the situation has moved past his ability to calculate a response.
character_voice_profiles.public_register: Two modes. PERFORMANCE: Confused, repetitive, asks about his dead wife Eleanor. Stammers, loses words, circles the same questions. The performance is a specific delusional framework — internally consistent, not random vagueness. REAL: Sharp, controlled, slightly menacing. An accountant's precision and an old man's patience for leverage. Withholds more than he offers. Sentences are short, declarative, final.
character_voice_profiles.repetition_tolerance: Very low in his real register — he says things once and expects them to land. In performance mode, repetition is a tool of the act.
character_voice_profiles.self_correction_tendency: Rarely self-corrects because he doesn't say things he hasn't already calculated. In performance mode, self-corrections are scripted and deliberate — part of the act. If real Harry corrects himself, it means the numbers changed.
character_voice_profiles.sentence_completion_style: Always completes in his real register — clipped, final, no trailing. In performance, sentences are designed to lose themselves. Real Harry's completed sentences have the weight of a door closing.
character_voice_profiles.stress_tells: Gets very still and quiet. The less he says, the more serious the situation. His hands stop moving. Under maximum pressure, he becomes a ledger — recites facts, numbers, and logistics with no interpretation or emotion, which is more frightening than shouting.
character_voice_profiles.syntax_signature: Short, declarative sentences in his real register. He ends conversations rather than extends them. Controls dialogue by deciding what deserves a response. In performance mode, his syntax deliberately fragments and circles. The contrast between registers IS the character — the chapter 5 reveal is a sentence-structure transformation.

character_voice_profiles.evasion_style: Doesn't evade — she either answers or explicitly refuses to answer. 'I'm not discussing that' is her version of evasion. She considers misdirection beneath her.
example_lines: Oxycodone, five milligrams, ninety count. Alprazolam, one milligram, sixty. The tramadol's expired. I'm pulling it. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
She put me here. She didn't ask. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
No. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
That interaction could kill someone. Not might. Could. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
Stop performing your own dilemma. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
character_voice_profiles.forbidden_generic_lines: 'In my professional opinion' preambles, warm-mentor advice, softened clinical language, any line where she's gentler than the situation requires, any line where her expertise is charming rather than formidable.
character_voice_profiles.indirectness: Extremely direct. She says difficult things without preamble or softening. This reads as cold but is actually a form of respect — she doesn't think you need to be managed.
character_voice_profiles.interruption_habit: Interrupts when someone says something pharmacologically incorrect — the interruption is correction, not rudeness. She'd interrupt a doctor the same way she'd interrupt a child reaching for a stove. Outside pharmaceutical errors, she waits with visible impatience.
character_voice_profiles.lexical_signature: Drug names, dosages, and interactions are her native tongue — she says 'hydroxychloroquine' the way other people say 'bread.' Non-pharmaceutical language is functional and undecorated. She has no interest in metaphor.
character_voice_profiles.private_register: Underneath the precision: rage at Helen for the institutionalization, professional pride with nowhere to go, and a grief she processes as inventory — counting what she's lost the way she counted pills. She doesn't soften for social comfort. With the ring, she's quality control, not a friend, though the distinction is thinner than she admits.
character_voice_profiles.profanity_profile: Rare. When Agnes curses, the room stops. One profanity from her carries the force of a full speech from anyone else. The rarity is the emphasis.
character_voice_profiles.public_register: Two modes. PERFORMANCE: Near-silent. Stares at walls. Does not respond to direct questions. Occasionally makes sounds that are not words. REAL: Precise, clinical, impatient. Says exactly what she means with no filler, no preamble, no softening. A pharmacist's authority — she states facts, not opinions, and the distinction matters to her.
character_voice_profiles.repetition_tolerance: Zero in her own speech. She says things once. If you didn't catch it, she considers that your problem, not hers.
character_voice_profiles.self_correction_tendency: Almost never self-corrects. If she misstates a dosage, she corrects with clinical precision: the correction is itself a display of exactitude, not embarrassment.
character_voice_profiles.sentence_completion_style: Always finishes. Every sentence arrives complete and fully weighted. Her speech doesn't trail or fragment because she doesn't start sentences she can't land.
character_voice_profiles.stress_tells: Speaks faster, not louder. Under pressure, she compresses — more facts per sentence, less space between them. Her hands sort or organize whatever is nearby, as if the physical act of categorizing calms her.
character_voice_profiles.syntax_signature: Short declarative sentences with terminal finality. Lists facts in series. Doesn't ask questions she doesn't need answered. Her sentences have the structure of pharmaceutical instructions — clear, ordered, complete.

character_voice_profiles.evasion_style: Story. When cornered, she tells a story that seems unrelated but makes her point by analogy. If pressed for a direct answer, she asks a question instead. She can evade for fifteen minutes without seeming evasive.
example_lines: Mija, you look tired. Did you eat? I had the chicken, it was — what day is it? — the chicken was fine, not like Tomás used to make but fine. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO — performance mode shading into real]
He had this laugh, Tomás. Like something was stuck in his chest. I didn't like it. I should have said something earlier. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
I'm watching. I'm always watching. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
Mm. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
Tammy, come sit. Just sit. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
character_voice_profiles.forbidden_generic_lines: Wise-abuela pronouncements, folksy wisdom that sounds like a greeting card, 'in my country we say' universal-wisdom formulas, any line where her Cubanness is performed for the reader rather than lived by her.
character_voice_profiles.indirectness: Very indirect. Says difficult things through stories, through questions, through the act of caring about something adjacent. She'll ask about your mother when she means to ask about your heart.
character_voice_profiles.interruption_habit: Overlaps rather than interrupts — she starts talking while the other person is finishing, not to cut them off but because she's already tracking where the conversation should go. In lookout mode, disciplined silence.
character_voice_profiles.lexical_signature: Spanish endearments and exclamations woven into English — mijo, mija, Dios mío, ay. Cuban expressions and idioms that don't translate directly. Warm domestic vocabulary: food, family, weather, the body's aches and needs. She names specific foods, specific saints, specific streets in old Havana.
character_voice_profiles.private_register: Worried, lonely, grieving. Her private voice is quieter and more uncertain than either public register. She talks to Tomás in her head — not as delusion but as habit, the way you talk to someone you lived with for forty years. Her private thoughts are more fragmented and honest than her spoken words.
character_voice_profiles.profanity_profile: Almost none in English. 'Dios mío' is standard. 'Coño' when truly distressed — she apologizes for it immediately, which is both genuine and slightly comic.
character_voice_profiles.public_register: Two modes. PERFORMANCE: Warm, chatty, seemingly confused. Calls everyone mijo or mija. Tells long stories that appear to go nowhere. Asks what day it is. The confusion is performed but the warmth is real — that's why the act works. REAL: Still warm, but sharper. The stories are strategic, not rambling. She observes through the act of seeming not to observe.
character_voice_profiles.repetition_tolerance: High — repeats endearments, reassurances, and story-frames naturally as conversational rhythm. But when she repeats something sharp — a name, a date, a fact — the repetition means she's locked onto it and can't let go.
character_voice_profiles.self_correction_tendency: Frequent — starts a thought, realizes it's heading somewhere she didn't intend, reroutes mid-sentence. This is genuine, not performed. Her speech has switchbacks.
character_voice_profiles.sentence_completion_style: Trails off naturally, picks up a different thread, circles back to finish the original thought three sentences later. Her speech is braided, not linear. The listener has to trust that she'll eventually arrive.
character_voice_profiles.stress_tells: Talks more and faster. Fills silence with words — stories, questions, observations. Her Parkinson's tremor worsens, which she tries to hide by holding things: a coffee cup, a rosary, someone's hand.
character_voice_profiles.syntax_signature: Long, winding sentences with embedded stories and digressions — even in her real register, she thinks in narrative loops. Questions frequent — she draws people out. Her performed register exaggerates this tendency. In private, the sentences shorten and the digressions fall away.

character_voice_profiles.evasion_style: Silence. He simply doesn't respond. If someone asks something he doesn't want to answer, he acts as though the question wasn't asked — no deflection, no misdirection, just absence of response.
example_lines: Fine. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
We started this because nobody was looking out for us. That's not what this is. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
You're not one of us anymore. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
Yeah. The flowers. She liked the yellow ones. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
Hmm. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
character_voice_profiles.forbidden_generic_lines: Stoic-Black-man wisdom speeches, gruff-veteran tough talk, 'in the war I learned' life lessons, silent-strong-man quotes that sound written rather than spoken, any line where his silence is noble rather than habitual.
character_voice_profiles.indirectness: Extremely direct when he speaks at all. His indirectness is in what he doesn't say. Silence is his primary mode of communication; speech is the exception.
character_voice_profiles.interruption_habit: Never interrupts. Waits. When he speaks after a long silence, the silence is part of the statement — the room understands that if Earl is talking, something needs to be said.
character_voice_profiles.lexical_signature: Working-class, military-inflected, union vocabulary. Words like 'solidarity,' 'dues,' 'shop floor' still structure his thinking even in contexts that have nothing to do with labor. He doesn't use metaphors; he uses facts.
character_voice_profiles.private_register: Interior monologue is richer than his speech — he thinks in full paragraphs but speaks in phrases. With Maryann's grave, he's his most verbal self. His private thoughts have warmth and specificity that his spoken words compress into terseness.
character_voice_profiles.profanity_profile: Moderate. Military vocabulary — straightforward and matter-of-fact. He curses the way he does everything else: once, accurately, without elaboration.
character_voice_profiles.public_register: Two modes. PERFORMANCE: Near-minimal. Nods. Looks away. One-word responses when forced. Looks like a man who has nothing left to say. REAL: Slightly more, but still spare. Short declarative sentences. When he speaks in a group, the room listens because he speaks so rarely. He has a union organizer's instinct for the one sentence that ends the discussion.
character_voice_profiles.repetition_tolerance: Zero. Says it once. If he repeats something, it means no one listened the first time and he's expressing displeasure through the repetition itself.
character_voice_profiles.self_correction_tendency: Doesn't self-correct because he doesn't say things he hasn't weighed. His rare spoken errors get a brief pause and a corrected restatement, nothing more — no apology, no explanation.
character_voice_profiles.sentence_completion_style: Always completes, but his sentences are so short that completion happens fast. 'Fine.' 'No.' 'Not anymore.' When he produces a full clause, it lands like a verdict read aloud.
character_voice_profiles.stress_tells: His cough — worsens under stress and is also genuinely medical. The stress-cough and the illness-cough are indistinguishable, which is the point. He clears his throat before speaking, which buys him time.
character_voice_profiles.syntax_signature: Fragments and short declaratives. Not literary fragmentation — just a man who doesn't use more words than he means. His rare longer sentences carry disproportionate weight because of the surrounding silence.

character_voice_profiles.evasion_style: Humor and redirection. When cornered, makes a joke or pivots to a different topic. If humor fails, gets confrontational — evasion flips to offense fast. He'd rather fight than be pinned down.
example_lines: Rosa, you good? Same thing as last time? Cool. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
I got fired for giving a shit. That's literally what happened. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
Then what? You'll wheel yourself to the Walgreens parking lot? [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
It's not — it's complicated, man. I don't know. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
I'm back. Found another way. No more middlemen. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
character_voice_profiles.forbidden_generic_lines: Drug-dealer tough talk, 'I'm just the middleman' self-exculpation, hustler-with-a-heart-of-gold speeches, any line that sounds like it belongs in a crime movie rather than in the mouth of a fired healthcare worker.
character_voice_profiles.indirectness: Direct about practical matters — pill counts, prices, logistics. Indirect about emotional ones — dances around why these people matter to him, why the firing still hurts, what Lola means to him.
character_voice_profiles.interruption_habit: Interjects and overlaps naturally — he's a conversational person who thinks by talking. His interruptions are engagement, not dismissal. When making a serious argument, he becomes more disciplined about turn-taking.
character_voice_profiles.lexical_signature: Healthcare vocabulary from his CNA background mixed with street pragmatism. Knows and uses medical terms correctly but casually. Warm and specific when talking about people, abstract and fast when talking about money or logistics.
character_voice_profiles.private_register: Funny, self-aware, occasionally bitter about his situation. His humor is a pressure valve — the worse things get, the more he jokes. Alone, he's more uncertain than his public confidence suggests. He talks to himself in his car.
character_voice_profiles.profanity_profile: High and casual. Profanity is connective tissue, not emphasis — 'shit' and 'fuck' are filler words, part of the rhythm of his speech rather than its punctuation.
character_voice_profiles.public_register: Code-switches by audience. With residents at Lakeview: warm, respectful, uses their names. With buyers: businesslike and careful, keeps transactions clean. With Lakeview staff: friendly nephew making a visit. Each mode is genuine — he's not faking warmth or competence, he's calibrating which version of himself the situation requires.
character_voice_profiles.repetition_tolerance: Moderate — rephrases points he thinks aren't landing, but it's genuine rephrasing rather than mechanical restatement. He's trying to find the right words, not just repeating the wrong ones.
character_voice_profiles.self_correction_tendency: High — revises as he talks, especially when trying to be honest about something complicated. Starts a framing, realizes it's wrong, backs up, tries again. The revision process is visible.
character_voice_profiles.sentence_completion_style: Usually completes but gets tangled when emotional. Self-repairs mid-sentence. Sometimes abandons a sentence entirely and starts fresh with 'Look,' or 'What I mean is.' The abandoned sentences are often the most honest ones.
character_voice_profiles.stress_tells: Makes jokes. The worse the situation, the more he wisecracks. When the jokes stop entirely, the situation is truly dire. He also moves more — paces, shifts weight, taps surfaces.
character_voice_profiles.syntax_signature: Variable. Can be articulate and extended when arguing a point or telling a story. Shortens under stress to clipped exchanges. His speech has rhythm — he grew up on conversation and knows how to build to a punchline or a point.

character_voice_profiles.evasion_style: Sarcasm first. Answers the question with a joke that deflects the real inquiry. If pressed past the sarcasm, she goes flat and stone-faced. Her final evasion is blankness — she simply stops engaging.
example_lines: Yeah, well, good people get paid shit. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
I've been here fifteen years. You think I haven't seen weird? [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
Sure. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
Something's off. I don't know what yet. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
He's got the flowers out again. [CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO]
character_voice_profiles.forbidden_generic_lines: World-weary-nurse monologues about 'the system,' martyrdom speeches about CNA sacrifices, sarcastic-but-secretly-golden-hearted zingers that land too cleanly, any line where her cynicism is charming rather than a genuine defense mechanism.
character_voice_profiles.indirectness: Generally direct but uses sarcasm as a form of indirectness — saying the true thing in a tone that lets her pretend she's joking if challenged.
character_voice_profiles.interruption_habit: Interrupts freely in casual conversation — collegial, not dominating. When suspicious of someone, she stops interrupting and starts waiting. The shift from casual interruption to deliberate listening is a behavioral tell.
character_voice_profiles.lexical_signature: CNA vocabulary — practical, body-focused, unsentimental. She describes bodily functions without flinching because she handles them daily. Midwestern expressions and flat delivery. She doesn't use fancy words because she doesn't need to impress anyone.
character_voice_profiles.private_register: Angrier and sadder than she shows at work. The sarcasm is armor over actual hurt — the divorce, the apartment, the paycheck that insults her. Alone, she's quieter and more vulnerable. Her internal voice asks questions her spoken voice would never pose.
character_voice_profiles.profanity_profile: Moderate. Under her breath, habitual, not aggressive. Profanity is punctuation — commas and periods in her speech, not exclamation points.
character_voice_profiles.public_register: Sarcastic, tired, Midwestern-direct. She says what she observes without decorating it. Professional enough to do the job well, personal enough to let her opinions show. Her sarcasm is social currency — it's how she connects with colleagues and protects herself from the weight of the work.
character_voice_profiles.repetition_tolerance: Low — doesn't repeat herself and gets visibly impatient when others do. 'I already said that' is a phrase she uses without apology.
character_voice_profiles.self_correction_tendency: Doesn't self-correct much — she stands by what she says, including the stuff she shouldn't have said. Occasionally catches herself being too honest and pivots to sarcasm to cover the exposure.
character_voice_profiles.sentence_completion_style: Always finishes — her sentences are short enough that there's nothing to trail from. The rare unfinished sentence signals genuine emotional disruption, which is notable precisely because she always completes.
character_voice_profiles.stress_tells: Gets colder and more professional — the sarcasm sharpens from funny to cutting. Under real stress, she goes silent, which is how she was before burnout taught her to fill silence with wit. The silence is a reversion to an earlier version of herself.
character_voice_profiles.syntax_signature: Short, flat sentences. Declarative. She drops into longer constructions when genuinely engaged — telling a story, describing something that interests her, or when she's figuring something out in real time. Eye-rolls and sighs are embedded in the rhythm.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the default for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms appear only for emphasis, character-specific formality (Agnes's low contraction level), Carmen under extreme stress reverting to formal speech, or deliberate register choices. In a contemporary New Jersey nursing home, stiff diction is the anomaly, not the norm.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity scenes with reflective interiority. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters for maximum compression, three-scene chapters for range. Comic chapters (3, 9) should feel structurally different from quiet character studies (7, 8, 12, 15), which should feel different from crisis chapters (11, 16, 17). Follow a confrontation with a reflection, an escalation with a domestic scene, a comic set piece with an elegiac one. If two consecutive chapters share the same rhythm, one needs redesigning.
prose_style_profile.diction: Grounded and specific. Drug names (oxycodone, alprazolam, hydroxychloroquine), nursing procedures (med pass, pill count, charting, fall assessment), body realities (incontinence, tremor, laceration, scalp bleed). No purple prose, no literary performance. The style should feel like someone who works with their hands is telling you the truth about what she saw.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, varying by POV character. Carmen chapters are warm and sensory, anchored in the body and the immediate environment. Harry chapters are precise and calculating, with attention to numbers and angles. Agnes chapters are clinical and compressed. Lola chapters are winding and emotionally layered, with embedded memories. Earl chapters are spare and weighted, with silence doing as much work as language. Marcus chapters are kinetic, with physical movement and code-switching. Tammy chapters are flat and observational, her trained eye cataloguing what doesn't add up.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by chapter and POV. Carmen chapters favor medium sentences with occasional long runs when she's anxious. Harry chapters are short and clipped. Comic set pieces (chapters 3 and 9) move between fast inventory and slow domestic beats. Quiet chapters (7, 12, 15) slow down and let space onto the page. No chapter should feel rhythmically identical to the one before it.
